feature_name: Dual operation mode: Root Complex and End Point
description: Supports operation as a PCIe Root Complex (RC) or End Point (EP), selectable via bootstrap pins and software. As EP it can operate as legacy or native PCIe end point. Software can override default mode through device registers. Mode determines configuration header type and permissible transactions. Enables flexible topologies in embedded systems.
dependencies: PCIESSMODE bootstrap; DEVSTAT (device-specific); LTSSM_EN=1
Remarks: Choose RC for host/root roles and EP for device roles; ensure matching configuration flows.

feature_name: Link width and speed (x1/x2, Gen1/Gen2 2.5/5.0 GT/s)
description: Implements a single bidirectional link up to ×2 lanes, operating at 2.5 or 5.0 GT/s per lane. Supports dynamic width conversion and lane reversal with automatic negotiation. Speed change to Gen2 is controlled by PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD after Gen1 link-up. Provides ultra-low TX/RX latency.
dependencies: REFCLK present; SerDes PLL lock; LINK training enabled; COMMON_CLK_CFG as needed
Remarks: Set LNK_MODE and DIR_SPD per board design; verify negotiated LINK_SPEED and width in LINK_STAT_CTRL.

feature_name: Max payload and read request sizes
description: Outbound max payload is 128 bytes; inbound max payload is 256 bytes; maximum remote read request size is 256 bytes. These limits affect throughput and DMA segmenting. Reads larger than limits are split by completers. Configure requester Max Payload/Request sizes in DEV_STAT_CTRL.
dependencies: Enumeration/config software sets MAX_PAYLD and MAX_REQ_SZ; peer capabilities
Remarks: Tune sizes to system interconnect and peer limitations for optimal performance.

feature_name: Endian conversion for inbound/outbound
description: Supports both little and big endian systems with programmable byte-swap on 1/2/4/8-byte boundaries. ENDIAN register selects mode when device is big-endian. Inbound and outbound conversions ensure data ordering compatibility with PCIe little-endian core.
dependencies: System endianness; program ENDIAN_MODE before transfers
Remarks: Select swap mode that matches software data layout; verify with test patterns.

feature_name: Outbound address translation (32 regions)
description: Maps internal bus address ranges to PCIe memory space using 32 equal-sized regions (1/2/4/8 MB). OB_SIZE sets region size; each region uses OB_OFFSET_INDEXn and OB_OFFSETn_HI to form 32/64-bit PCIe addresses. Large transactions must not cross region boundaries.
dependencies: Configure OB_SIZE; program OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI; OB_XLT_EN=1
Remarks: Plan contiguous mappings to avoid boundary violations; keep region count and size aligned with DMA windows.

feature_name: Inbound address translation (4 regions, BAR association)
description: Translates accepted PCIe addresses to internal memory via four regions keyed to BAR matches. IB_BARn selects BAR; IB_STARTn_{HI,LO} sets window base; IB_OFFSETn sets internal base. BAR0 is always mapped to application registers and cannot be remapped.
dependencies: Program BARs; IB_x registers; IB_XLT_EN=1; proper enumeration
Remarks: Use BAR1+ regions for data windows; in 64-bit mode pair BARs; respect BAR0 exception for control access.

feature_name: Configurable BAR mask and BAR behavior override (DBI_CS2)
description: Allows EP/RC software to preconfigure BAR size/type via BAR Mask registers overlaid on BARs. Enable write access using CMD_STATUS.DBI_CS2, then program BARx_MASK and BARx. BAR0 mask is limited; BAR0 always maps application registers.
dependencies: DBI_CS2=1 prior to writes; enumeration order; readback verification
Remarks: Clear DBI_CS2 after setup; validate masking by test writes to BARs as recommended.

feature_name: Configuration and IO access windows (Address Space 0)
description: Provides 16 KB Address Space 0: 4 KB application registers, 4 KB local config, 4 KB remote config, and 4 KB RC-only IO window. Remote cfg/IO accesses require a live link; burst is not supported on these windows.
dependencies: LTSSM link up; CFG_SETUP; IOBASE programmed (RC)
Remarks: Guard against accesses when link is down; treat these regions as non-cacheable and 32-bit only.

feature_name: Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI) generation and reception
description: Generates and receives up to 32 MSI vectors in RC and EP. EP generates MSI via memory write to RC’s MSI_IRQ; RC receives MSI mapped to MSIn IRQ status with EOI handshake. MSI capability and address/data are set in MSI_CAP, MSI_LOW32/UP32, and MSI_DATA.
dependencies: MSI_CAP.MSI_EN=1; program MSI address/data; MSIn enable/status; IRQ_EOI
Remarks: Ensure INTx disabled when MSI enabled; clear status and write EOI before re-arming vectors.

feature_name: Legacy INTx interrupt support (RC receive, EP generate)
description: RC receives INTA/B/C/D legacy messages; EP can assert/deassert a selected INTx via EP_IRQ_SET/CLR. Interrupt events are exposed via LEGACY_X IRQ status/enable registers. Only one legacy interrupt can be pending per function until deasserted.
dependencies: Configure INT_PIN; enable LEGACY_X IRQ; handle EOI and status clears
Remarks: Account for race between deassert arrival and EOI; probe downstream device cause prior to EOI.

feature_name: Error, power/reset, and link event interrupts (14 total lines)
description: Provides consolidated interrupt events: system/fatal/nonfatal/correctable errors, AXI bridge fatal, PME/turnoff/ack, hot reset/link down, and MSI groups. Managed via ERR_IRQ_* and PMRST_* registers and application IRQ_EOI.
dependencies: Enable ERR/PMRST bits; service and clear W1C status; write IRQ_EOI
Remarks: Map critical events to CPU interrupt controller with appropriate priorities.

feature_name: Advanced Error Reporting (AER) and logging
description: Implements PCIe baseline and Advanced Error Reporting with mask/severity controls, ECRC enable, and 4×DW header logging. Root Complex has Root Error Command/Status and source ID capture. Software can selectively mask or classify errors as fatal/nonfatal and generate SERR/PME.
dependencies: Enable DEV_STAT_CTRL *_RP; PCIE_UNCERR/_MASK/_SVRTY; PCIE_CERR/_MASK; PCIE_ACCR; ROOT_ERR_CMD
Remarks: Disable ECRC if peer lacks support (see note); poll ROOT_ERR_ST and inspect HDR_LOGx for triage.

feature_name: ECRC generation and checking
description: Hardware can generate and verify End-to-End CRC on TLPs when enabled. Controlled by PCIE_ACCR ECRC_GEN_EN and ECRC_CHK_EN. Use only when both link partners support ECRC to avoid false errors.
dependencies: Peer capability; consistent configuration on both ends
Remarks: For RC mode to EPs without ECRC, clear ECRC_EN bits to prevent spurious error handling.

feature_name: Power management: device and link (ASPM L0s/L1, D-states)
description: Supports D0/D1/D2/D3hot/D3cold device states and Active State Power Management L0s/L1. Link and device power states interact per spec; L2 is not supported. Software controls via PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT and LINK_STAT_CTRL ACTIVE_LINK_PM and COMMON_CLK_CFG.
dependencies: Platform power policy; PM_CAP and PM_CTL_STAT; enumerated ASPM; common clock if SSC
Remarks: Balance latency vs. power; ensure common REFCLK for ASPM with SSC to minimize training overhead.

feature_name: DMA through VBUSM master/slave (no internal DMA)
description: Provides 128-bit VBUSM slave (outbound) and master (inbound) ports for data moves. External EDMA or CPU initiates transfers; approx. 85% of 2.0/4.0 Gbps per lane achievable accounting for 8b/10b. EDMA examples show memory write/read setup.
dependencies: Configured address translation; EDMA channels; bus mastering enabled
Remarks: Throttle sizes to 128B boundaries; align bursts; ensure BUS_MASTER (STATUS_COMMAND) is set before issuing.

feature_name: PIPE and PHY loopback modes
description: Supports PIPE loopback (master/slave roles) and PHY loopback for at-speed self-test without a link partner. Controlled via PL_LINK_CTRL.LPBK_EN and SerDes lane CFG registers for PHY loopback, with forced LTSSM states using PL_FORCE_LINK.
dependencies: Disable normal traffic; set LPBK_EN or SerDes RX/TX loopback; force link to POLL_ACTIVE as required
Remarks: Set loopback before any traffic to avoid sequence mismatch; exclude BAR0/limit filters that divert traffic.

feature_name: Reset and hot reset control
description: Supports POR, system warm reset, and software-initiated downstream hot reset. RSTCMD.INIT_RST issues Hot Reset; FLUSH_N indicates pending transactions. After reset, wait ≥100 ms before PCIe transactions; some sticky bits retain state.
dependencies: Quiesce traffic; check FLUSH_N=0; reinitialize link and configuration
Remarks: Honor post-reset delays (100 ms to 1.5 s worst-case) and re-enumerate as needed.

feature_name: Clocking and SerDes PLL configuration/status
description: Uses differential REFCLK (typically 100 MHz) into SerDes PLL; supports common clock and SSC systems. Configure PLL multipliers and options via chip-level PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL; monitor lock via PCIE_SERDES_STS.LOCK; set DIR_SPD for Gen2.
dependencies: Stable REFCLK; program MPY; ENPLL=1; wait for LOCK; set COMMON_CLK_CFG if no common clock
Remarks: Place and filter REFCLK carefully to meet jitter; gate link training until PLL lock is asserted.

feature_name: Transaction and burst alignment rules
description: Outbound transactions limited to ≤128 bytes and must be 8-byte aligned; misaligned writes limited to 120 bytes. Only incremental bursts supported; fixed/wrap bursts cause undefined behavior. Read interleaving is avoided if sizes ≤ Max Transaction Size.
dependencies: DMA and CPU initiators must honor size/alignment; Max TX size config
Remarks: Program DMA aCnt/bCnt to 128B segments; ensure byte enables are contiguous per byte strobe rules.

feature_name: Master privilege and priority control
description: Inbound master transactions can require supervisor privilege and carry a PRIVID. PRIORITY register sets MST_PRIV, PRIVID, and inbound master priority (0 highest to 7 lowest). Helps isolate access classes and prioritize PCIe-initiated memory traffic.
dependencies: System privilege model; interconnect QoS settings
Remarks: Use higher priority for real-time inbound traffic; restrict register access to supervisor mode when needed.

feature_name: Application and debug register access (BAR0 mapping)
description: 4 KB application registers exposed locally and via BAR0 to RC/EP; RC can access EP’s application registers through upper 1 KB of configuration space. DEBUG0/1 provide LTSSM state and link diagnostics; Appendix A maps LTSSM codes.
dependencies: BAR0 accessibility; link up for remote access
Remarks: Use LTSSM_STATE (DEBUG0[4:0]) during bring-up; protect BAR0 window from unintended remote writes.

feature_name: Performance tuning: ACK/NFTS/timers and SKP
description: Programmable ACK frequency, number of fast training sequences, and replay/latency timers allow optimizing link behavior. Control via ACK_FREQ, PL_ACKTIMER, and SYM_NUM (SKP_COUNT/TS counts). Can reduce retraining overhead in stable common-clock systems.
dependencies: Stable link; COMMON_CLK_CFG for tighter timing; comply with spec minimums
Remarks: Avoid overly aggressive settings that violate timing; verify with link partner and compliance test.

feature_name: Message transmit and TLP filtering controls
description: Software can inject PCIe Messages using PL_OMSG and PL_LINK_CTRL.OMSG_REQ. Extensive filter masks control acceptance/handling of CFG/IO/MSG, completion attribute checks, UR behavior, FC watchdog, and vendor messages via SYMTIMER_FLTMASK and FLT_MASK2.
dependencies: Filter defaults; link training up; message formats per spec
Remarks: Use with care in RC mode; enabling drops may impact compliance—document any deviations.

feature_name: Completion timeout support (ranges and disable)
description: Supports configurable completion timeout ranges and optional disable for requesters issuing their own TLPs. Controlled by DEV_CAP2 (supported ranges) and DEV_STAT_CTRL2 (CMPL_TO). Software must not set timeouts below 10 ms.
dependencies: Requester role; sensible timeout policy
Remarks: Prefer default 50 ms–50 s range; only tighten where end-to-end path is proven.

feature_name: Power domain and module state transitions
description: Turning PCIe power domain and module off resets the block; traffic must be quiesced on both ends before power down. On power-up, software must reinitialize and retrain the link; no extra delay beyond PLL lock is required before use.
dependencies: PSC sequencing; quiesced link; PLL re-init
Remarks: If hung due to improper power-off, a device POR may be needed; follow PSC GOSTAT sequencing.
